fresh_facts.claim: The U.S. deployed at least two carrier strike groups (USS Gerald R. Ford and USS Abraham Lincoln) to the Middle East region, with a third preparing to sail.
Operation Epic Fury involved over 1,000 targets struck in initial 24 hours, using F-16s, F-15Es, B-52s, Tomahawk missiles, and cyber operations.
U.S. forces sank over 30-43 Iranian vessels including a drone carrier in Operation Epic Fury.
By early March, over 3,000 targets had been struck in Operation Epic Fury, targeting missile infrastructure, IRGC, and nuclear-related sites.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei died on the first day of the war with the US and Israel.
Mojtaba Khamenei was officially named Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts following Ali Khamenei's death.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated on March 10 that renewed US talks are unlikely.
IRGC spokesmen rejected ceasefires and threatened Gulf oil shipments.
U.S. President Trump demanded Iran's unconditional surrender, defined by the White House as neutralizing its missile and nuclear threats.
Airstrikes on March 9-10 targeted Tehran fuel storage and oil facilities, causing economic disruption and driving up global oil prices.
A 10-day internet blackout has been imposed affecting Iran's economy and communications.
IDF strikes on March 10 destroyed most internal security and Basij assets in Ilam Province targeting protest suppression infrastructure.
Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones that were intercepted by U.S. and allied defenses in Qatar, UAE, Kuwait, Jordan, and Saudi Arabia.
U.S. forces achieved air and sea dominance within six days of Operation Epic Fury per CENTCOM.
Reza Pahlavi declared himself leader of a potential transitional government but lacks any formal mandate or broad support.
Parliament speaker Mohammad-Baqer Qalibaf affirmed no ceasefire pursuit by Iran.
Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Saar stated Israel seeks no endless war and will align with the US on stopping.
IDF reported striking Iranian special forces, IRGC, ballistic missile units, and Basij in Tehran and Tabriz on March 10.
Iran has fired approximately 300 ballistic missiles at Israel with diminishing volleys over the conflict period.
Over 5,000 targets have been struck by March 10 including command centers, exacerbating economic strain.
Nighttime patrols with armored vehicles in Tehran aim to intimidate residents as of March 6.
IRGC support for Mojtaba Khamenei as successor was reported around March 7 amid resistance from figures like Ali Larijani.

regime_fall.current_status: Over 5,000 cumulative strikes have devastated Iranian military infrastructure and economic assets, while the newly-named Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei faces deepening internal divisions, a 10-day internet blackout, fuel shortages from targeted strikes on oil facilities, and a demoralized security apparatus following targeted destruction of protest suppression assets.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Mojtaba Khamenei was officially named Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts on March 9-10, 2026, following Ali Khamenei's death on March 1, amid contested succession and continued multi-front military pressure exceeding 5,000 strikes targeting command centers and economic infrastructure.
